title: Eurovision Song Contest Starts in Asia for the First Time
summary: The Eurovision Song Contest, a big music competition from Europe, is now coming to Asia for the first time. Ten countries in Asia, like South Korea, Thailand, and the Philippines, will join. They will have their own shows to pick songs, and then a big final will be in Bangkok on November 14. Eurovision started in 1956 and is the world's longest-running music contest. Martin Green, a director, said it's special to include Asia because it has lots of culture and talent. People on social media are excited, with some saying the Philippines will win. This is not the first time Eurovision has tried to grow; it had an American version in 2022, but not many people watched. Organizers hope Asia will do better because people there love music, like K-pop from South Korea. Other countries have their own music styles too.

a city in Thailand where the final will be
websites and apps where people share things online

questions.question: How many countries in Asia are joining the Eurovision Song Contest?
Where will the grand final of Eurovision Asia be held?
When did the Eurovision Song Contest first start?
What music style from South Korea is mentioned as popular in Asia?
What happened to the American version of Eurovision in 2022?

background_read: The Eurovision Song Contest is a fun music competition that started in Europe in 1956. Countries send singers to perform songs, and people vote for their favorites. It's known for colorful costumes and exciting shows. Now, it's expanding to Asia, where countries like South Korea and Thailand have their own music styles. This is a big step to include more cultures and talents from around the world.
Article_Structure: WHO: Ten countries in Asia, including South Korea, Thailand, and the Philippines, and Martin Green, a director.
WHAT: The Eurovision Song Contest is launching its first Asia edition with a grand final in Bangkok.
WHERE: In Asia, with the final in Bangkok, Thailand.
WHY: To include Asia's rich culture and talent, and because people there love music.

perspectives.description: Many people are happy about Eurovision coming to Asia, seeing it as a chance to showcase diverse music and cultures.
